title: Management Summary — AI / LLM Exposure fragment
description: Structured data for the optional '### AI / LLM Exposure' callout in the Management Summary. Names the architectural AI/LLM risks the report already articulates (OWASP LLM Top-10 lens), so the fact that the system embeds an LLM/agent — and its headline risks — is visible at executive level instead of scattered across §7 control prose. Authored by the threat-renderer ONLY when the system has an LLM/AI surface (the OWASP LLM Top-10 lens fired, i.e. KNOWN_LLM_PATTERNS != none); omit the file entirely otherwise (the section then renders nothing — zero cost for non-LLM repos).

summary.description: Optional 1-sentence executive framing of the AI surface — what the LLM/agent does and where its trust boundary sits (e.g. 'A chat assistant relays user input to an external model API and renders the completion back to the browser').

ai_risks.description: Architectural AI/LLM risks, most severe first. Each is a real risk evidenced in the report, mapped to an OWASP LLM Top-10 category — NOT speculation.

owasp_llm_id.description: Optional OWASP Top-10 for LLM Applications (2025) category id. Rendered as a leading badge.

name.description: Canonical risk name — e.g. 'Prompt Injection', 'Excessive Agency', 'Improper Output Handling', 'Model Supply Chain', 'System Prompt Leakage', 'Unbounded Consumption'. A named AI risk, not a finding restatement.

severity.description: Optional traffic-light cue driving the leading emoji. Defaults to red. Use yellow for a partially-mitigated risk.

description.description: 1-2 sentence design-review prose: what untrusted input reaches which AI sink and why it is a structural risk. References components/files GENERICALLY (the line-by-line detail lives in the linked findings and §7).

affected_components.description: Component-ids (or {id, name} dicts) carrying the risk. The renderer enriches bare ids to '[C-NN](#c-nn) — Name'.

findings.description: 1-4 representative findings the risk manifests in. Each {ref, label}; renderer emits '[ref](#anchor) — label'.
